value="aind-data‑transfer-service" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="80" width="195" x="507.5" y="-510" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="UL96ZQgQ9Tx9a3Nr9wBj-19" parent="SpeKbif7ejOxNLxw_tv2-1" style="text;html=1;whiteSpace=wrap;strokeColor=none;fillColor=none;align=left;verticalAlign=top;rounded=0;fontSize=14;" value="<div>To initiate an upload, an Authorized User or Watchdog Service:</div><div><br></div><div>1. Compiles some metadata or extracted metadata as json files. Users need to provide the files needed to construct instrument and acquisition files, then move their data asset to VAST including the metadata or extracted metadata json files.<br></div><div><br></div><div>2. Send a compress and upload request to the aind-data-transfer-service. The current request includes a subject_id, acq_datetime, job_type, and desired S3 bucket.<br></div><div><br></div><div>3. The transfer service then:</div><div>- Runs the aind-metadata-mapper library on an HPC node. This creates a acquisition, instrument, subject, procedures, and data_description metadata files and saves it to a temp staged directory on VAST.<br></div><div>- Compresses the data</div><div>- Uses the aind-metadata-mapper library to construct a processing metadata file and saves it to the staged directory.<br></div><div>- Uploads the data and metadata files to S3.<br></div><div>- Uses aind-data-access-api to register the asset that was uploaded to S3. This scrapes the S3 location for the json files, combines them into a single record, and saves the record to DocDB.</div><div>- Registers the S3 folder as an asset on Code Ocean<br></div><div>- Triggers Code Ocean pipelines </div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="660" width="260" x="900" y="-720" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="XjCrOu-fNeJbmDTpR4Ur-2" edge="1" parent="SpeKbif7ejOxNLxw_tv2-1" source="UL96ZQgQ9Tx9a3Nr9wBj-4" box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;

fill: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 193px; height: 1px; padding-top: 284px; margin-left: 429px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 258px; height: 1px; padding-top: 41px; margin-left: 822px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 14px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
div.div: To initiate an upload, an Authorized User or Watchdog Service:

1. Compiles some metadata or extracted metadata as json files. Users need to provide the files needed to construct instrument and acquisition files, then move their data asset to VAST including the metadata or extracted metadata json files.
2. Send a compress and upload request to the aind-data-transfer-service. The current request includes a subject_id, acq_datetime, job_type, and desired S3 bucket.

- Runs the aind-metadata-mapper library on an HPC node. This creates a acquisition, instrument, subject, procedures, and data_description metadata files and saves it to a temp staged directory on VAST.
- Uses the aind-metadata-mapper library to construct a processing metadata file and saves it to the staged directory.
- Uploads the data and metadata files to S3.
- Uses aind-data-access-api to register the asset that was uploaded to S3. This scrapes the S3 location for the json files, combines them into a single record, and saves the record to DocDB.
- Registers the S3 folder as an asset on Code Ocean
